ops.title: Fleet plugin.json autobump CI (cc-skills @plugin-v1): bump lane auto-commits patch bumps for unbumped content drift, guard lane for binary-pinned plugins; deterministic script-ref (job_workflow_sha AND job_workflow_ref are empty cross-repo); tools/plugin-d
ops.body:
Shipped 2026-07-22 (AUDIT-PLF-2026-07, cc-notes log faa50fb4): the plugin-cache staleness incident class ("content change without a plugin.json bump never reaches consumers") is closed mechanically fleet-wide.

**Machinery** — `yasyf/cc-skills/.github/workflows/plugin-autobump.yml` (movable tag `plugin-v1`) + `.github/scripts/plugin_autobump.py` (PEP 723, stdlib; 15 pytest cases under plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/). Detection is state-based and idempotent: baseline = newest first-parent commit where the parsed top-level `version` VALUE changed; drift = tree diff baseline..HEAD over the plugin root (excludes `.claude-plugin/`, `.github/`, `.claude/`, `.gitignore`, and any NESTED sibling plugin roots) OR structural plugin.json change ignoring version. Non-guarded drift → one bot commit `chore(plugins): autobump … [skip ci]` (patch bump only; semantic bumps stay human). Mutation refuses outside CI (`GITHUB_ACTIONS` gate, `AUTOBUMP_FORCE=1` override) after an incident where a local verification run pushed to main.

**Lanes** — 13 repos call it: bump lane (cc-skills' 12 cache-only plugins + cc-notes, cc-guides, write-like-me, experiment-at-home, slop-cop, cc-transcript, getaway) armed on push + weekly strict cron (`23 10 * * 1`); guard lane (binary-pinned codex/cc-context/cc-review/cc-present/cc-runtime + release-synced captain-hook) never auto-bumps — drift warns on push, red only on schedule or an explicit `strict` dispatch input, and a RELEASE is the ship vehicle (auto-bumping would 404 install-binary.sh's version-pinned download). cc-orchestrate is exempt (version null → SHA-keyed cache). repo-bootstrap stamps the caller into new plugin repos (`templates/plugin/github/workflows/autobump.yml`).

**Why:** the load-bearing GHA lesson — `github.job_workflow_sha` AND `github.job_workflow_ref` both arrive EMPTY on cross-repo reusable-workflow calls (observed live twice, contrary to docs), so the script checkout uses no context magic: same-repo callers pin `github.sha`, cross-repo callers pin the `script-ref` input (default `plugin-v1`, the tag the workflow ships under). Machinery updates = move the tag.

**How to apply:** don't hand-sweep version bumps or fear the bot commits — `[skip ci]`, GITHUB_TOKEN pushes trigger nothing, re-runs see the bump commit as the new baseline (idempotent). A guarded plugin showing persistent red on the weekly strict run means its owner owes a release. Companion tools in cc-skills: `tools/plugin-doctor.py` (derives the exact `plugin@marketplace --scope` update per install record — bare names and default user-scope are the [[plugin-scope-churn]]-adjacent footgun) and `tools/plugin-cache-prune.py` (+ weekly launchd agent `com.yasyf.plugin-cache-prune`, installed on yBook-Pro; yasyf-home pending task 4e3cfc03). Related: [[plugin-cache-keys-on-version]], [[plugin-cache-never-prunes — see task log]].
